name: Atlassian Data Center MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Atlassian Data Center products (Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket Data Center) — the self-hosted enterprise edition. Enables AI agents to interact with self-hosted Atlassian instances for issue management, code reviews, documentation, and project tracking without relying on Atlassian Cloud.
use_cases: Querying and managing Jira Data Center issues from project management agents
Searching Confluence Data Center documentation from knowledge retrieval agents
Managing Bitbucket Data Center pull requests and code reviews from DevOps agents
Integrating self-hosted Atlassian tools into AI-driven enterprise workflows
not_for: Atlassian Cloud users (use Atlassian Cloud MCP instead)
Teams without self-hosted Atlassian Data Center instances
Small teams — Data Center is enterprise-scale and costly
best_when: An enterprise using self-hosted Atlassian Data Center (for data residency, compliance, or customization reasons) wants AI agents to interact with their Jira, Confluence, or Bitbucket instances.
avoid_when: You use Atlassian Cloud. This MCP targets Data Center (self-hosted) specifically. Cloud and Data Center APIs differ — use the right MCP for your deployment.

auth.notes: Atlassian Data Center personal access tokens or Basic auth. Configure least-privilege Atlassian project/space permissions for agent service accounts.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — MCP server is open source; Atlassian DC licensing is separate enterprise cost
pricing.paid_tiers: None for MCP server; Atlassian DC license is significant enterprise expense
pricing.notes: MCP server is free open source. Atlassian Data Center requires expensive per-user licensing (thousands/year).

performance.rate_limits: Bounded by your Atlassian DC instance capacity and configuration

known_agent_gotchas: Atlassian DC API differs from Cloud — ensure MCP is targeting your specific DC version
Data Center instances vary in configuration — test against your specific setup
Enterprise data: Jira/Confluence may contain sensitive project and business information
Self-hosted availability depends on your infrastructure — no external SLA
DC API deprecations follow DC release cycle — check compatibility on DC upgrades
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Enterprise Atlassian data (issues, docs, code) may be sensitive. Use least-privilege service accounts. Self-hosted — TLS configuration is operator responsibility.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise data sensitivity. Self-hosted — security depends on your Atlassian DC configuration. Least-privilege service accounts essential.
